steps.title: Find the needed verb
steps.content: The subject of the clause is tales of whose exploits, and myths is the object. The blank must supply a finite main verb so the clause is complete.

steps.title: Reject nonfinite forms
steps.content:
• Choice A creates a participial phrase, not a complete clause.
  • Choice C is an infinitive, so it cannot serve as the main verb.
  • Choice D is a participle, leaving the clause without a finite verb.

steps.title: Complete the clause
steps.content: Eventually became is a simple past-tense verb, producing the complete idea that the tales eventually became myths. The answer is B.
